title: System Configuration
description: System-scope configuration (config/system.json). The system-wide layer of the layered configuration model (RD-5): structured JSON validated by this published schema. All Tier-3 instance constants (reviewer ids, channel-role bindings) land here, never in code. Spec ref: release-spec.md §11.2.

schema_version.description: Version of this config shape, for migration handling (§11.1).

mode.description: Publish posture ladder. Fresh installs default to SAFE (fail-closed). LIVE requires an operational project plus an explicit config change.

reviewers.description: The approval allowlist (DD-17). MUST be non-empty with at least one reviewer holding approve rights. Every approval/edit decision is attributed to an entry here; edits require the same rights as approvals.

budget.description: Spend governance (DD-18). REQUIRED: the engine refuses LIVE mode without it. Caps bind engine-metered spend and run dispatch only; chain-seat LLM spend is capped at the host runtime (see docs/cost.md). A cap breach forces the project to paused (§15.4).

currency.description: ISO 4217 currency code for all cap values.

monthly_cap.description: Hard monthly ceiling on engine-metered spend. NOT a whole-system ceiling unless the host runtime reports chain spend.

daily_cap.description: Hard daily ceiling on engine-metered spend.

per_item_generation_limit.description: Maximum engine-metered spend permitted producing a single content item.

indexing_requires_estimate.description: When true (required), engine index-library and engine calibrate MUST present a pre-run cost estimate and require confirmation before spending.

publish.description: Publish posture (the canonical home of the diagnostic publish-flag overrides). draft_only true means approved posts land as drafts the operator publishes manually (the second gate).

draft_only.description: When true (default), publisher handoff creates a draft; the operator publishes it manually.

auto_publish_allowed.description: Global gate for per-brand auto-publish. Even when true, each brand×platform must independently earn the trust state in brand.json (§15.5).

approval_surface.description: The approval surface adapter and its channel-role bindings. Channel ids are Tier-3 instance constants supplied by the operator; templates ship placeholders only.

adapter.description: Approval-surface adapter id. v1 reference implementation is discord.

channels.description: Channel-role bindings by id. Required roles: content-review, content-published, content-ops, media-bank. trend-readout is optional.

scheduler.description: Trigger schedule. Safety posture never lives in scheduler wrappers; it lives here in declared config.

ttl_sweep_interval_minutes.description: Approval-card TTL sweep interval in minutes.

tick_enabled.description: Enable the optional fine-grained calendar-tick trigger. Ships off by default; the daily kickoff is the canonical trigger.

cooldown.description: Media reuse cooldown defaults (per-brand overridable in brand.json).

hard_days.description: Hard floor: an asset or its derivatives MUST NOT be reused more often than this.

gate.description: Deterministic-gate tunables. The engine ships generic day-one defaults in code (engine/gate/pre-gate-lint.js, §10.3); the operator/maintainer's calibrated values live here and are NOT shipped (DD-9).

variant_distinctness.description: Variant-duplication thresholds (rule.core.variant). Two variants fire LINT.VARIANT_DUP when their n-gram body similarity reaches jaccard_threshold or they share the opener window. Shipped defaults are generic starting points, not calibrated values.

shingle_n.description: Word n-gram (shingle) size for body-overlap similarity.

jaccard_threshold.description: Body-overlap similarity at/above which two variants are too similar. Generic default; tune per brand voice.

opener_chars.description: Leading-character window compared for identical openers.

trend.description: Trend-card TTL. The token 'freshness-window-bound' means TTL is inherited from the originating trend report's freshness window.

evergreen_escalate_after.description: Age after which an evergreen card escalates rather than silently expiring.

retention.description: Corpus retention windows + purge schedule (RD-9). The operator is the data controller; engine purge-corpora honors retention_class.

raw_corpus_days.description: Default retention (days) for retention_class:standard / untagged raw third-party corpora before purge eligibility.

transient_days.description: Retention (days) for retention_class:transient corpus items (the shortest window). engine purge-corpora purges transient items older than this.

purge_schedule.description: Cadence at which engine purge-corpora is expected to run (e.g. daily, weekly).

observability.description: Health-surface configuration: digest timing and stall thresholds (§13).

stall_thresholds.description: Thresholds that drive heartbeat/stall alerts. Silent death MUST NOT be the failure presentation.

no_content_produced_hours.description: Alert if no content has been produced within this many hours.

queue_age_alert_hours.description: Alert if cards remain pending past this age.

undelivered_card_backlog.description: When undelivered-card backlog exceeds this count, new generation pauses (degraded-mode guard).

paused.description: Mirror of the PAUSED sentinel. When true, all autonomous loops are paused (kill switch, §15.4).

id.description: Reviewer identity on the approval surface (Tier-3). Resolvable by the surface adapter.

rights.description: Granted rights. At least one reviewer in the allowlist MUST have 'approve'.

channel_id.description: Approval-surface channel identifier (Tier-3 instance constant). Operator-supplied; templates use placeholders such as <CHANNEL_ID>.

duration.description: Duration token, an integer followed by a unit (h hours, d days, m minutes).
